title: Today's AI Brief: How we used Gemini to build Google I/O 2026
summary: Google's use of Gemini for I/O 2026 and Alphabet's $85B stock sale for AI
imageAlt: Image from source article for How we used Gemini to build Google I/O 2026
imageCaption: How we used Gemini to build Google I/O 2026
imageSourceTitle: How we used Gemini to build Google I/O 2026

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI Brief. Let's dive into the latest news. Google just shared how they used their AI model, Gemini, to build their I/O 2026 event. From the jellyfish pre-show to the 'TPU Training Day' film, Gemini was behind the scenes, helping make it happen. This is pretty cool, as it shows how AI can be a game-changer for event production.

According to Google's blog post, Gemini helped with various aspects of the event. This shows how AI can be used to streamline and enhance event production. It's a practical example of AI in action, making a big event like I/O more efficient. I'm curious to see how this tech will be used in future events, maybe even in our daily lives.

In other news, Alphabet, Google's parent company, just completed a record-breaking $85 billion stock sale. The goal was to boost their AI business. This massive raise signals that investors are hungry for AI-related offerings. It's clear that the market is excited about the potential of AI. And who can blame them? AI is changing the game, and it's only getting better.

How we used Gemini to build Google I/O 2026 is also worth a quick mention. Marvin Chow VP, Marketing Share x.com Facebook LinkedIn Mail Copy link Your browser does not support the audio element. Listen to article This con... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
